title: Filtering, selecting, and editing feeds
text: Filter functions keep related identifiers consistent across feed. filter_stop() filter_time() deliberately retain partial trips. useful corridor, express-service, time-window experiments. retained trip can therefore begin end different stop source feed. Service patterns describe exact sets services active calendar dates. Filtering pattern retains precisely represented dates services. explicit pattern, filter_servicepattern() uses frequent active pattern. Use filter_date() dates inside feed calendar. Calendar calendar-date tables rebuilt represent retained dates.
code: route_ids <- gtfs$routes$route_id[1:2] route_feed <- filter_route(gtfs, route_ids)  service_id <- gtfs$trips$service_id[1] service_feed <- filter_service(gtfs, service_id)  time_feed <- filter_time(gtfs, from = "06:00:00", to = "09:00:00") stop_ids <- gtfs$stops$stop_id[1:5] partial_feed <- filter_stop(gtfs, stop_ids) typical_feed <- filter_servicepattern(gtfs) #> Warning: GTFSwizard: no `servicepattern` supplied; using `servicepattern-1`, #> the most frequent pattern. active_date <- gtfs$dates_services$date[   lengths(gtfs$dates_services$service_id) > 0 ][1] date_feed <- filter_date(gtfs, active_date)

title: Service analysis and visualization
text: GTFS service_id identifies one service calendar. Several IDs can operate date. GTFSwizard assigns service_pattern dates exact set active services. Consequently, one service_id can belong several patterns services operating alongside change. pattern_frequency number dates represented exact active service set. frequent active pattern therefore useful default typical day, necessarily weekday interpreted feed calendar.

title: Service analysis and visualization
text: Frequency counts scheduled departures. Headway measures elapsed minutes successive service instances comparable group. Route-level results retain direction_id available. Common method names use underscores: by_trip returns one observation per trip; by_route aggregates route, direction, service pattern applicable; by_hour aggregates scheduled service hour; detailed returns stop-call interval-level observations. title: Creating, Exploring and Manipulating GTFS Files
text: concept service_pattern GTFSwizard helps address common limitation GTFS: identify recurring combinations services active date. pattern exact set service_id values operating calendar date, independent route. wizardgtfs objects, dates_services table extended feature consolidates dates associated service_ids single, organized table. table standard typical GTFS files added specifically wizardgtfs objects. dates_services table structured date associated list service_ids representing transit services operating specific day. Essentially, unique list service_ids observed across dates defines distinct service pattern. common observe least 3 service patterns: weekdays, saturdays sundays. Dates inside feed calendar range active services treated explicit empty service set: trip counts 0, calendar-level pattern labeled "service". Structure dates_services: date dates_services table associated list service_ids, capturing set services active particular day. Defining Service Patterns: unique service_pattern identified unique combination service_ids operating given date. instance, two dates share exact service_ids, considered part service_pattern. -service dates: date active service_ids represented "service" pattern calendar-level outputs get_servicepattern() service_id = NA. trip-bearing pattern used filter_servicepattern(). can check service_pattern using get_servicepattern() function. analytical functions account service patterns, including get_frequency() plot_routefrequency(). Patterns ordered frequent (useful typical-day default) less frequent, route-frequency plots compare represented patterns.  can use plot_calendar() check number trips along calendar get better sense service_pattern rationale. Dates without active service appear 0 trips trip-count mode "service" service-pattern mode.

title: Creating, Exploring and Manipulating GTFS Files
text: Filtering tools allow GTFS data restricted service patterns, dates, service IDs, route IDs, trip IDs, stop IDs, time ranges. filter_ functions retain relevant records GTFS relationships. filter_servicepattern(): Filter specified active service patterns. Defaults frequent active pattern (typical day) none provided. "service" pattern describes calendar days without trips accepted filter. filter_date(): Filter data specific dates, returning services active dates. filter_service(): Filter specific service IDs retain. filter_route(): Filter route ID. Set keep = TRUE retain specified routes keep = FALSE exclude . filter_trip(): Filter trip ID. Set keep = TRUE retain specified trips keep = FALSE exclude . filter_stop(): Keep stop calls requested stop IDs. Trips may remain partial, supports route network experiments. filter_time(): Keep stop calls inside specified time range (). Trips cross time boundary remain partial trips.
code: # Filter by service pattern filtered_gtfs <- GTFSwizard::filter_servicepattern(for_bus_gtfs, "servicepattern-2")  # Filter by specific date filtered_gtfs <- GTFSwizard::filter_date(for_bus_gtfs, "2021-02-10")  # Filter by route ID, retaining only specified routes filtered_gtfs <- GTFSwizard::filter_route(for_bus_gtfs, for_bus_gtfs$routes$route_id[1:2])  # Filter by trip ID, excluding specified trips filtered_gtfs <- GTFSwizard::filter_trip(for_bus_gtfs, for_bus_gtfs$trips$trip_id[1:2], FALSE)  # Filter by time range filtered_gtfs <- GTFSwizard::filter_time(gtfs = for_bus_gtfs, "06:30:00", "10:00:00")  # Spatial filter using filter_stop spatial.filter <- GTFSwizard::get_shapes_sf(for_bus_gtfs$shapes)  stops <- sf::st_filter(GTFSwizard::get_stops_sf(for_bus_gtfs$stops),                        spatial.filter) |>           dplyr::pull(stop_id)  filtered_gtfs <- GTFSwizard::filter_stop(for_bus_gtfs, stops)

title: Creating, Exploring and Manipulating GTFS Files
text: GTFSwizard provides functions edit GTFS data directly. delay_trip() function allows users apply delay specific trips. split_trip() function divides trip valid consecutive parts. Trips can split split + 1 approximately equal parts, selected internal stop IDs using stops argument. can useful analyzing partial routes simulating route adjustments. edit_speed() function adjusts travel speeds stops GTFS dataset modifying trip durations based specified speed multiplier. allows selective adjustments specific trips stops applies changes globally. set_dwelltime() function overwrites selected dwell times retaining arrival edited stop shifting downstream times. trip end time may therefore change. edit_dwelltime() function scales selected dwell times applies downstream propagation. merge_gtfs() function combines two GTFS files, allowing integration distinct GTFS datasets single dataset. Export resulting feed write_gtfs(), writes standard GTFS .zip archive requested path.
code: # Delay trips by 5 minutes (300 seconds) delayed_gtfs <- GTFSwizard::delay_trip(for_bus_gtfs, trip = for_bus_gtfs$trips$trip_id[1:2], duration = 300)  # Split a trip in 3 sections (2 splits) split_gtfs <- GTFSwizard::split_trip(for_bus_gtfs, trip = for_bus_gtfs$trips$trip_id[1:2], split = 2)  # Split a trip at selected stop IDs split_at_stop_gtfs <- GTFSwizard::split_trip(for_bus_gtfs, trip = for_bus_gtfs$trips$trip_id[1], stops = for_bus_gtfs$stop_times$stop_id[2])  # Merge two GTFS files into one merged_gtfs <- GTFSwizard::merge_gtfs(for_bus_gtfs, for_rail_gtfs)  # Double the speed of all trips faster_gtfs <- GTFSwizard::edit_speed(for_rail_gtfs, factor = 2)  # Set and edit dwell times for specific trips gtfs <- set_dwelltime(for_rail_gtfs,                                                trips = for_rail_gtfs$trips$trip_id[1:100],                    stops = for_rail_gtfs$stops$stop_id[1:20],                    duration = 10)  gtfs <- edit_dwelltime(gtfs,                                                        trips = for_rail_gtfs$trips$trip_id[1:100],                    stops = for_rail_gtfs$stops$stop_id[1:20],                    factor = 1.5)  get_dwelltimes(gtfs, method = 'detailed') GTFSwizard::write_gtfs(for_bus_gtfs, 'path-to-file.zip')

title: Creating, Exploring and Manipulating GTFS Files
text: functions described facilitate analysis, simulation, evaluation public transportation systems. assist replication real-world transit interventions, enabling researchers, planners, policymakers test refine system modifications controlled efficient manner. Key applications outlined along potential uses addressing typical challenges opportunities public transit systems. Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) Exclusive Lanes: BRT systems often rely exclusive corridors reduce travel times enhance reliability. Using edit_speed(), users can represent changes towards smaller travel times corridors adjusting travel speeds. addition, edit_dwelltime() allows representation optimized boarding alighting processes, reflecting reduced dwell times stations due level boarding, pre-payment mechanisms, increased operational efficiency; Frequency Modifications: Frequency adjustments among common transit interventions. using filter_trip() select trips doubled, delay_trip() change first departure_time, merge_gtfs() add original GTFS, users can represent increased frequencies, reflecting higher service levels. Conversely, filter_trip can used reduce service frequencies, allowing evaluation cost-saving measures temporary schedule adjustments; Route Segmentation Partial Adjustments: split_trip(), users can divide existing routes smaller segments, enabling partial route adjustments. particularly relevant studies assessing feasibility feeder services, route rationalization, service redundancy elimination. Express Services: Transit stops significantly influence travel times, operating costs, network coverage. Using edit_dwelltime() filter_stop() can subtract dwell times total trip durations remove unused stops, representing introduction express services.

title: GTFS Data for Fortaleza (Rail System), Brazil — for_rail_gtfs
text: object class wizardgtfs, consisting multiple data frames: agency Data frame 1 row 7 columns, providing information transit agency, including agency name, URL, timezone, language, contact details. calendar Data frame 1 row 10 columns, detailing service availability day week, along start end dates service. calendar_dates Data frame 26 rows 3 columns, listing specific dates exceptions (e.g., holidays) modify usual service pattern. routes Data frame 3 rows 9 columns, listing route details route ID, short long names, route type, colors associated route. stops Data frame 39 rows 10 columns, containing information stop, including stop ID, name, location (latitude longitude), additional details. stop_times Data frame 3,420 rows 10 columns, detailing arrival departure times trip, along stop sequences stop IDs. trips Data frame 215 rows 7 columns, providing trip-specific information trip ID, headsign, direction, associated service ID, route ID, shape ID. shapes Data frame 80 rows 5 columns, representing spatial paths routes using latitude, longitude, point sequence, cumulative distance traveled.

what: Calculate Distances in GTFS Data — get_distances
title: Calculate Distances in GTFS Data — get_distances
text: `get_distances` function calculates distances within `wizardgtfs` object based various methods. Depending `method` chosen, can calculate average route distances, trip-specific distances, detailed distances stops.

title: Calculate Distances in GTFS Data — get_distances
code: get_distances(gtfs, method = "by_trip", trips = "all")

title: Calculate Distances in GTFS Data — get_distances
text: gtfs GTFS object, ideally class `wizardgtfs`. class, converted. method character string indicating calculation method. Choices : "by_route" Calculates average distances route. "by_trip" Calculates distances trip, associating trip ID total distance. "detailed" Calculates detailed distances consecutive stop trips. computationally intensive option may take several minutes complete. trips character vector trip IDs consider. set ``, includes trips.

title: Calculate Distances in GTFS Data — get_distances
text: data frame calculated distances based specified method: `method = "by_route"` Returns summary route direction trip counts, average distance, service pattern, pattern frequency. `method = "by_trip"` Returns trip distance route, direction, service pattern, pattern frequency. `method = "detailed"` Returns data frame columns: `shape_id`, `from_stop_id`, `to_stop_id`, `distance`.

title: Calculate Distances in GTFS Data — get_distances
text: function calls specific sub-functions based selected method: - "by_route": Calculates average distances per route. - "by_trip": Calculate distances per trip. - "detailed": Calculates detailed stop--stop distances within route. Note method may slow large datasets. Legacy dotted values remain accepted. invalid `method` provided, function defaults `"by_trip"` issues warning.

title: Calculate Distances in GTFS Data — get_distances
code: # Calculate average route distances distances_by_route <- get_distances(gtfs = for_rail_gtfs, method = "by_route", trips = 'all')  # Calculate distances by trip distances_by_trip <- get_distances(gtfs = for_rail_gtfs, method = "by_trip", trips = 'all')  # \donttest{ # Calculate detailed distances between stops detailed_distances <- get_distances(gtfs = for_rail_gtfs, method = "detailed", trips = 'all') #> GTFSwizard: calculating detailed stop-to-stop distances. # }

title: Calculate Dwell Times in GTFS Data — get_dwelltimes
text: `get_dwelltimes` function calculates dwell times within `wizardgtfs` object using different methods. Depending selected `method`, can provide average dwell times per route, per trip, hour, detailed dwell times stop.

title: Calculate Dwell Times in GTFS Data — get_dwelltimes
code: get_dwelltimes(gtfs, max_dwelltime = 90, method = "by_trip", ...)

title: Calculate Dwell Times in GTFS Data — get_dwelltimes
text: gtfs GTFS object, ideally class `wizardgtfs`. , converted. max_dwelltime Numeric. Maximum allowable dwell time seconds. method character string specifying calculation method. Options include: "by_hour" Calculates average dwell time per hour day across trips. "by_route" Calculates average dwell time route. "by_trip" Calculates average dwell time trip. "detailed" Calculates detailed dwell times stop within every trip. ... Supports legacy argument `max.dwelltime`.

title: Calculate Dwell Times in GTFS Data — get_dwelltimes
text: data frame containing dwell times based specified method: `method = "by_hour"` Returns data frame columns: `hour`, `trips`, `average.dwelltime`, `service_pattern`, `pattern_frequency`. `method = "by_route"` Returns `route_id`, optional `direction_id`, `trips`, `average.dwelltime`, `service_pattern`, `pattern_frequency`. `method = "by_trip"` Returns `route_id`, `trip_id`, optional `direction_id`, `average.dwelltime`, `service_pattern`, `pattern_frequency`. `method = "detailed"` Returns `route_id`, `trip_id`, optional `direction_id`, `stop_id`, `hour`, `dwell_time`, `service_pattern`, `pattern_frequency`.

title: Calculate Dwell Times in GTFS Data — get_dwelltimes
text: function calls specific sub-functions based selected method: - "by_hour": Calculates average dwell time hour day. - "by_route": Calculates average dwell times across route. - "by_trip": Calculates mean dwell time trip. - "detailed": Calculates departure minus arrival stop call. Legacy dotted values remain accepted. invalid `method` specified, function defaults `"by_trip"` provides warning.

title: Calculate Dwell Times in GTFS Data — get_dwelltimes
code: # Calculate dwell times by hour dwelltimes_by_hour <- get_dwelltimes(gtfs = for_rail_gtfs, max_dwelltime = 120, method = "by_hour")  # Calculate dwell times by route dwelltimes_by_route <- get_dwelltimes(gtfs = for_rail_gtfs, max_dwelltime = 90, method = "by_route")  # Calculate dwell times by trip dwelltimes_by_trip <- get_dwelltimes(gtfs = for_rail_gtfs, max_dwelltime = 45, method = "by_trip")  # Calculate detailed dwell times between stops detailed_dwelltimes <- get_dwelltimes(gtfs = for_rail_gtfs, max_dwelltime = 60, method = "detailed")

title: Calculate Travel Times with RAPTOR Algorithm — tidy_raptor
text: `tidy_raptor` function calculates travel times set origin stops reachable stops within GTFS dataset. uses RAPTOR (Round-Based Public Transit Routing) algorithm `tidytransit` package integrates GTFSwizard framework.

title: Calculate Travel Times with RAPTOR Algorithm — tidy_raptor
code: tidy_raptor(   gtfs,   min_departure = "0:0:0",   max_arrival = "23:59:59",   dates = NULL,   stop_ids,   arrival = FALSE,   time_range = 3600,   max_transfers = NULL,   keep = "all",   filter = TRUE,   separate_starts = FALSE )

title: Calculate Travel Times with RAPTOR Algorithm — tidy_raptor
text: gtfs GTFS object, preferably class `wizardgtfs`. , function attempt convert using `GTFSwizard::as_wizardgtfs()`. min_departure string representing earliest departure time, "HH:MM:SS" format. Defaults `"0:0:0"`. max_arrival string representing latest arrival time, "HH:MM:SS" format. Defaults `"23:59:59"`. dates One service date. `NULL`, latest active service date used. stop_ids character vector stop IDs journeys start (end, `arrival = TRUE`). arrival Logical. `FALSE` (default), journeys start `stop_ids`. `TRUE`, journeys end `stop_ids`. time_range Either range seconds (numeric) vector minimal maximal departure time (e.g., `c(0, 3600)` `"HH:MM:SS"`) describing journey window. max_transfers Maximum number transfers allowed. Defaults `NULL` (limit). keep One `""`, `"shortest"`, `"earliest"`, `"latest"`. Determines journeys retain: - `""`: journeys returned (default). - `"shortest"`: journeys shortest travel time. - `"earliest"`: Journeys arriving stops earliest. - `"latest"`: Journeys arriving stops latest. filter Logical. Apply `min_departure`, `max_arrival`, `dates` routing. separate_starts Logical. Keep results origin start time separately; passed [tidytransit::raptor()].

title: Calculate Travel Times with RAPTOR Algorithm — tidy_raptor

title: GTFSwizard 1.2.1
text: Organized package website workflow, added task-based function reference sections, added vignettes getting started, service analysis, filtering editing, interactive dashboard. Standardized public method values underscores: by_trip, by_route, by_hour, by_stop, by_shape. Legacy dotted values remain accepted. Standardized public argument names including file_path, gtfs_x, gtfs_y, max_dwelltime, min_length, compatibility aliases previous names. Improved tidy_raptor() validation, transfer handling, date selection, output column names. Added planning indicators explore_gtfs(), including scheduled headway, commercial speed, service span, vehicle-hours, peak fleet, stop spacing, route connectivity. Calendar service-pattern summaries now represent dates active service explicitly: trip counts 0, service pattern labeled "service".

title: GTFSwizard 1.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-22 plot_routefrequency() now uses route-hour-service pattern tile plot limits default display busiest routes top_n, improving readability large feeds. explore_gtfs() adds route-count control route-frequency plots keeps filtered service service-pattern selections aligned.

title: GTFSwizard 1.1.1
text: create_gtfs() introduced. CRAN resubmission release following archival caused archived hrbrthemes dependency. dependency removed, package dependency set substantially reduced. GTFS editing selection filter_stop() filter_time() now preserve partial trips experimentation instead restoring complete stop sequences. selection() now supports grouping columns computed groups style similar dplyr::group_by(), continuing support logical spatial selections without altering GTFS tables. Plotting dashboard Fixed repeated warnings -Inf values shape_dist_traveled contains missing values. explore_gtfs() removes duplicated system information, assigns distinct route colors, filters non-finite plotting values. static plots use shared visual theme palette. Package quality Added Francisco Moraes de Oliveira Neto author. Expanded integrity, round-trip, selection, spatial, plotting, regression tests. Reviewed exported function documentation GTFS logic current GTFS Schedule reference.

title: GTFSwizard 1.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-03-15 Now featuring travel time matrices, trip speed dwell-time editing, corridors, hubs, ! New functions tidy_raptor() estimates travel time matrices gtfs feeds; edit_speed() adjusts travel speed GTFS dataset; edit_dwelltime() modifies dwell time trips; set_dwelltime() overwrites dwell time trips; get_hubs() estimates high integration potential stops; get_corridor() suggests “right--way” transit corridors; plot_corridor() plots suggested corridors get_corridor() function; get_1stdeparture() retrieves start timepoint trip; latlon2epsg() determines appropriate EPSG code given sf object. New methods get_headways() now accepts ‘method = “by_stop”’ ‘method = “by_shape”’. get_frequency() now accepts ‘method = “by_shape”’ ‘method = “by_stop”’. Bug fix & improvements Better column naming; Minor bug fixed filter_date() get_shapes_sf(); get_headways() output fixed; split_trip() can now used filter_time(); get_speeds(), get_durations() get_distances() now accept trips argument.

title: GTFSwizard 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-11-11 Initial CRAN submission.
